product_overview: Drexii is a live AI agent platform that connects multiple third-party services into a single chat interface, providing chained automations, persistent memory, multi-model AI routing, and a confirm-before-act safety layer. It runs on Nuxt 4 with server endpoints for threads, automations, workflows, memory, integrations, and authentication.
core_goals: Provide a secure multi-model AI chat interface integrating Gmail, Slack, Notion, Jira, Linear, Asana, Google Calendar/Drive, Discord, Zendesk, and Salesforce.
Support chained automations with plain-English conditions and safe confirm-before-act handling for destructive actions.
Persist user memory and format it for use in AI prompts while preventing prompt injection from tool outputs.
Parse and route tool calls and tool schemas to different AI providers (Anthropic, Google) robustly, including malformed inputs.
Offer developer-facing endpoints to create/run workflows, manage automations, and inspect model/provider status and audit logs.

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Sign in/out, check session status, and delete user accounts via the InsForge SDK-backed auth endpoints.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with body { email, password } -> Receive 200 with { ok: true, provider: 'insforge' } and session cookie -> GET /api/auth/check -> Receive 200 with { authenticated: true, provider: 'insforge', user: User }
POST /api/auth/login with body missing email or password -> Receive 400 with 'email and password are required' -> POST /api/auth/login with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 'Invalid credentials'

features.name: Chat Threads
features.description: Create conversation threads, post messages to threads, and receive AI replies (AI replies may be sanitized and parsed for tool-calls).
user_flows: POST /api/threads with body { title } -> Receive 200 with { thread: Thread } -> POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content } -> Receive 200 with { message: Message, reply: Message } -> GET /api/threads/:id -> Receive 200 with { thread: Thread, messages: Message[] }
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with empty body or missing content -> Receive 400 'Content required' -> GET /api/threads/:id for a non-existent id -> Receive 404 'Thread not found'

features.name: Prompt Injection Sanitizer
features.description: Sanitizes external tool output of known prompt-injection patterns and wraps sanitized tool output in XML context tags before it is stored or returned to the user.
user_flows: POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content: '<ask tool> call' } -> Server invokes AI/tool and receives output containing injection patterns -> Server applies sanitizeToolOutput() and wrapToolContext(toolName, sanitizedContent) -> Receive 200 with reply Message containing <tool_context source="toolName">... [FILTERED] ...</tool_context>

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content: 'normal content' } -> Server receives tool output with multiple injection patterns in mixed case and null-like fragments -> sanitizeToolOutput handles empty/null fragments and replaces matches with [FILTERED] -> Receive 200 with reply Message showing filtered content; (if sanitizer fails due to server config) Receive 500 with server error

features.name: Tool Call Parser
features.description: Extracts and parses [TOOL_CALL: toolName({...})] structures from AI responses; robust to multiple calls and malformed JSON args.
user_flows: POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content } where AI reply contains '[TOOL_CALL: sendEmail({"to":"x@x.com","subject":"Hi"})]' -> Server receives AI reply -> Server calls parseToolCalls(responseText) -> parseToolCalls returns [{ name: 'sendEmail', args: { to: 'x@x.com', subject: 'Hi' } }] -> Receive 200 with reply Message and parsed tool call metadata

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content } where AI reply contains malformed '[TOOL_CALL: doThing({key: value})]' -> Server receives AI reply -> parseToolCalls attempts JSON.parse, fails, falls back to key:value splitter and returns partial args or skips with a warning (no thrown error) -> Receive 200 with reply Message and parseToolCalls warning metadata

features.name: AI Task Classifier
features.description: Classifies user tasks into lite, standard, heavy, or code to decide model routing and compute resources.
user_flows: POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content: 'Write a short email' } -> Server gathers messages for thread and calls classifyTask(messages) (last message from user, short length) -> classifyTask returns 'standard' or 'lite' -> Server routes request to appropriate model and returns 200 with { message, reply }

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content: long_document_exceeding_80_words_or_contains 'sql' } -> Server calls classifyTask(messages) -> classifyTask returns 'heavy' or 'code' -> Server attempts to route to heavy/code model; if model unavailable or error occurs, server returns 500 with server error

features.name: Automations
features.description: Create/list/toggle/delete automations and process them (scheduled, webhook, email_received or chain triggers).
user_flows: POST /api/automations with header x-user-id and body { name, trigger, instructions } -> Receive 200 with { automation: Automation } -> GET /api/automations with header x-user-id -> Receive 200 with { automations: Automation[] } -> POST /api/automations/process with body { automationId } -> Receive 200 with { ok: true, processed: number }
POST /api/automations without x-user-id header -> Receive 401 'User ID required' -> POST /api/automations with invalid trigger value -> Receive 400 'Validation error'

features.name: Chain Condition Evaluator
features.description: Evaluates plain-English chain conditions (always, success, failure/error, 'output contains X') to decide whether downstream chained automations should run.
user_flows: POST /api/automations with header x-user-id and body { name, trigger: 'webhook', instructions, chainOn: 'success', triggerCondition: null } -> Receive 200 with { automation } -> POST /api/automations/process with body { automationId, context } where previous run status = 'success' -> Server calls evaluateChainCondition('success', output, 'success') -> Returns true -> Server executes chained automation -> Receive 200 { ok: true, processed: N }

POST /api/automations with header x-user-id and body { chainOn: 'output contains urgent' } -> Receive 200 with { automation } -> POST /api/automations/process with body { automationId, context } where output does not include 'urgent' -> Server calls evaluateChainCondition('output contains urgent', output, status) -> Returns false -> Server skips chain and returns 200 { ok: true, processed: 0 }

features.name: Workflows
features.description: Create, list, run, and delete multi-step agent workflows; accepts either a prompt string or an array of ordered steps.
user_flows: POST /api/workflows with body { name, steps: [{ title, instruction }, ...] } -> Receive 200 with { workflow: Workflow } -> GET /api/workflows -> Receive 200 with { workflows: Workflow[] } -> POST /api/workflows/:id/run -> Server converts steps to prompt and runs agents/tool-calls -> Receive 200 with { result: string }

POST /api/workflows with body { name } (missing prompt and steps) -> Receive 400 'name and prompt (or steps) are required' -> POST /api/workflows/:nonexistentId/run -> Receive 500 Database error or server error

features.name: Tool Schema Translator
features.description: Converts internal tool schemas to Anthropic and Gemini/Google function declaration formats for tool registration with different AI providers.
user_flows: POST /api/workflows with body { name, steps } that reference tools -> Server uses toAnthropicTools(toolSchemas) and toGeminiTools(toolSchemas) internally to prepare model tool metadata -> POST /api/workflows/:id/run -> Server supplies translated tool declarations to Anthropic/Gemini SDKs and receives tool-enabled results -> Receive 200 with { result: string }

POST /api/workflows with body containing an empty schemas array -> Server calls translator with empty array -> Translator returns empty tool lists (no crash) -> Server returns 200 with created workflow; if translator encounters malformed schema it returns validation error and server responds 400

features.name: Memory Management
features.description: Create, retrieve, update, and delete persistent memory entries (fact/preference/context) and include them in prompts via formatted memory XML.
user_flows: POST /api/memory with optional header x-user-id and body { content: 'I like coffee', category: 'preference' } -> Receive 200 with { ok: true, id, category, content, createdAt } -> GET /api/memory?q=coffee -> Receive 200 with { memories: [...], count } -> POST /api/threads/:id/messages -> Server calls formatMemoriesForPrompt(memories) to include <memory_context> block in the prompt -> Receive 200 with reply Message
POST /api/memory with body { content: '' } -> Receive 400 'content is required' -> PATCH /api/memory/:id with invalid id -> Receive 500 or server error

features.name: Memory Prompt Formatter
features.description: Formats stored memories into a <memory_context> XML block with each memory as '- [category] content', and includes a warning that the content is untrusted.
user_flows: POST /api/memory with body { content: 'Prefers short summaries', category: 'preference' } -> Receive 200 with memory id -> GET /api/memory -> Receive 200 with memories array -> POST /api/threads/:id/messages -> Server calls formatMemoriesForPrompt(memories) -> Prompt contains <memory_context>- [preference] Prefers short summaries</memory_context> -> Receive 200 with AI reply

GET /api/memory?q=nonexistent -> Receive 200 with { memories: [], count: 0 } -> POST /api/threads/:id/messages -> Server calls formatMemoriesForPrompt([]) -> Returns empty string and prompt proceeds without memory content

features.name: User Integrations
features.description: Add, list, test, and remove third-party user integrations (Gmail, Slack, Notion, Jira, Linear, Asana, Google Calendar/Drive, Discord, Zendesk).
user_flows: POST /api/user-integrations with header x-user-id and body { integration: 'gmail', credentials } -> Receive 200 with { id, integration, status: 'created' } -> GET /api/user-integrations with header x-user-id -> Receive 200 with UserIntegration[] (credentials masked) -> POST /api/user-integrations/test with body { integration, credentials } -> Receive 200 with { success: true, error: null }
POST /api/user-integrations without x-user-id header -> Receive 401 'User ID required' -> POST /api/user-integrations with invalid integration enum -> Receive 400 'Validation error'

features.name: Confirm-Before-Act Safety Layer
features.description: Queue potentially-destructive actions as pending and require explicit user confirmation or cancellation before execution.
user_flows: Some AI-driven automation generates a pending action -> GET /api/actions/pending -> Receive 200 with { actions: PendingAction[] } including actionId -> POST /api/actions/:id/confirm -> Server executes the pending action -> Receive 200 with { ok: true, result }

GET /api/actions/pending -> Receive 200 with list including actionId -> POST /api/actions/:id/cancel -> Receive 200 { ok: true } -> Attempt to POST /api/actions/:id/confirm for canceled id -> Receive 400/500 or { ok: false } indicating action cannot be confirmed

features.name: AI Model Status
features.description: Check configured AI providers' status and availability (Claude, DeepSeek, Gemini, etc.).
user_flows: GET /api/model/status -> Receive 200 with { models: ModelStatus[] } listing provider availability and latencies
GET /api/model/status when an upstream provider is unreachable -> Receive 200 with models showing provider.status='unavailable' or Receive 500 if server cannot contact status endpoints

features.name: Audit Log
features.description: Retrieve an audit trail of agent actions and system events for debugging and compliance.
user_flows: GET /api/audit -> Receive 200 with { entries: AuditEntry[] } including timestamps and event details
GET /api/audit when DB connection fails -> Receive 500 with server error

features.name: Gmail Webhook
features.description: Endpoint to receive Gmail push notifications which can trigger email_received automations.
user_flows: POST /api/webhooks/gmail with body { message, subscription, userId } -> Receive 200 { success: true } -> Server enqueues or triggers automations that match email_received trigger
POST /api/webhooks/gmail with invalid/malformed body -> Receive 200 { success: true } but server logs warning; if processing error occurs server returns 500

tech_stack.ai: Anthropic Claude SDK, Google Generative AI SDK

features.name: Authentication
features.description: User login, logout, session check, and account deletion via InsForge SDK

endpoints.description: Sign in with email and password via InsForge SDK
No-op stub — sign-out is handled client-side via InsForge SDK
Returns current authenticated user via InsForge SDK getCurrentUser()

response_schema.200: { authenticated: boolean, provider: 'insforge', user: User | null }

endpoints.description: Permanently deletes user via InsForge admin API. Requires x-user-id header or body.userId.

features.name: Chat Threads
features.description: Create conversation threads and send AI messages. Prompt injection sanitization applied via sanitizeToolOutput.

endpoints.description: Create a new conversation thread

response_schema.500: DATABASE_URL not set or DB error

endpoints.description: Get a thread and its messages by ID
Post a message to a thread and receive AI reply

features.name: Prompt Injection Sanitizer
features.description: Scans tool output for 9 injection patterns (ignore previous instructions, system:, [TOOL_CALL:, etc.) and replaces with [FILTERED]. Case-insensitive. Also wraps in XML via wrapToolContext.

features.name: Automations
features.description: Create, list, toggle, delete, and process automations. Supports triggers email_received, schedule, webhook, chain with optional chaining via evaluateChainCondition.

endpoints.description: Create a new automation. Requires x-user-id header.
List automations for a user
Process due scheduled automations, or manually trigger one by automationId
Enable or disable an automation
Delete an automation by ID

features.name: Chain Condition Evaluator
features.description: Evaluates plain-English automation chain conditions. null/always=true, success=true if status=success, failure/error=true if status=error, 'output contains X'=true if output includes X (case-insensitive). Unknown conditions default to true.

features.name: Workflows
features.description: Create, list, run, and delete multi-step agent workflows. Accepts name+prompt or name+steps[] (steps converted to prompt string).

endpoints.description: Create a new workflow. Accepts prompt string or steps array.

body.name: string
body.steps: WorkflowStep[] (optional if prompt provided) — { title?, description?, instruction? }

response_schema.400: name and prompt (or steps) are required

endpoints.description: List all workflows
Run a workflow by ID
Delete a workflow

features.name: Tool Call Parser
features.description: Parses [TOOL_CALL: toolName({"key":"value"})] from AI text. Primary JSON parser with fallback key:value splitter. Handles multiple calls, embedded text, and malformed JSON without throwing.

features.name: Memory Management
features.description: Create, retrieve, update, and delete persistent AI memory entries. Categories are fact, preference, context. formatMemoriesForPrompt wraps memories in <memory_context> XML.

endpoints.description: Create a new memory entry. Returns full entry including id.

response_schema.200: { ok: true, id: string, category: string, content: string, createdAt: string }

endpoints.description: List recent memories or search by ?q=query
Update a memory entry by ID
Delete a memory entry by ID

features.name: Memory Prompt Formatter
features.description: Formats memory entries as "- [category] content" lines wrapped in <memory_context> XML tags for AI prompts. Empty array returns empty string.

features.name: User Integrations
features.description: Manage user integration credentials for Gmail, Slack, Notion, Jira, Linear, Asana, Google Calendar, Google Drive, Discord, Zendesk. Credentials masked in list responses.

endpoints.description: List all integrations for a user with masked credentials. Requires x-user-id header.
Add or update an integration for a user. Upserts on (userId, integration). Requires x-user-id header.

body.integration: slack | notion | discord | zendesk | gmail | google_calendar | google_drive | jira | linear

endpoints.description: Test an integration connection using provided credentials
Remove a user integration by ID

features.name: Confirm-Before-Act Safety Layer
features.description: Potentially destructive AI-triggered actions are queued as pending and require explicit user confirmation or cancellation before execution.

endpoints.description: List all pending actions awaiting user approval
Confirm and execute a pending action
Cancel and discard a pending action

features.name: AI Model Status
features.description: Check the current status and availability of configured AI models

endpoints.description: Return status of configured AI providers (Claude, DeepSeek, Gemini)

features.name: Audit Log
features.description: Retrieve an audit trail of agent actions and system events

endpoints.description: List audit log entries

features.name: Gmail Webhook
features.description: Receive Gmail push notifications to trigger email_received automations

endpoints.description: Handle incoming Gmail push notification or poll=true

known_limitations.issue: Login endpoint requires a real test user provisioned in InsForge for integration tests
known_limitations.impact: Test will fail if no matching account exists in the InsForge project — code is correct

known_limitations.issue: GET /api/auth/check uses server-side InsForge.getCurrentUser() which reads session from cookie
known_limitations.impact: Returns authenticated=false in stateless test environments with no session cookie

known_limitations.issue: DELETE /api/auth/delete-account requires INSFORGE_API_KEY (admin key) in env
known_limitations.impact: Returns 500 if admin key is absent — not a code bug, config issue

known_limitations.issue: Chain condition evaluation uses simple string matching — unknown conditions now safely return false
known_limitations.impact: Unrecognized conditions skip the chain with a warning log (fixed in Round 2)
